hypothesis: If adaptive, multi-metric diversity constraints (similarity, fragment, and n-gram thresholds with dynamic penalties) are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission, then the generated molecule sets will exhibit higher chemical diversity (lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, higher unique-fragment percentages) without a significant reduction in ΔE_SF-based reward compared to unconstrained runs, because penalizing overrepresented structures steers exploration toward under-sampled regions while maintaining optimization pressure.
